v. a. 1. To check; bring to a stand; cause to stop. Phr. To stop the way. Pahye meṅ oṭ aṛā de . Put a scotch under the wheel. 2. To interfere; intermeddle; thrust one's nose in; mix oneself up with. Kisī ke phaṭe meṅ kyoṅ pāoṅ aṛāte ho? lit . Why do you put your foot in another's 3. To cork or fill up; bung up; ram; close; plug; block up. Is bil meṅ roṛā aṛā de . 4. ( Double entendre ) To rub against to shove in; to effect penetration. Rāt bhar khūb aṛāyā . (Slang). 5. To thrust; shove; push against. Dīwār meṅ aṛā dȳā . He pushed him against the wall. 6. To suspend; put off; postpone. 7. To oblige; compel; force ( dabānā ). Apnā rupayā to aṛā-kar le lȳā . He took his money by force.

v. a. S. उत् + डी to fly upward. 1. To fly; let fly; uncage. Kabūtar, yā tukkal uṛānā . 2. To fly away with; carry through the air. Na mānā, par usne uṛāyā use, Parastān meṅ lā biṭhāyā use . Mīr Hasan. 3. To carry off (moisture); to dry; dry up. Is āṅdhī ne gazoṅ āl uṛā dī . 4. Met . To ride, or drive very hard or fast. Kyā ghoṛā uṛāë jātā hai! 5. To run away with; kidnap; elope with. 1. Ranḍī ko uṛā le gayā . 2. Raqībon ne jo dekhā, ye uṛā-kar le chalā us ko, Pukārā yeh koī, "kaisā huā aṅdher āṅdhī meṅ!" Āṅdhī . Nazīr. 6. To unfold; unroll; unfurl (the sail, flag). 1. Pāl uṛānā . 2. Jhanḍā uṛānā . 7. To drive or frighten away; scare. Khet pe se janāwar uṛāë de . Rus. 8. To scatter; disperse; throw; diffuse. Abīr, gulāl, yā khush-bū uṛānā . 9. To winnow; separate the husk from the corn ( barsānā ). 10. To spread abroad, or circulate. Afwāh uṛānā . 11. To waste; dissipate; lavish; squande…

v. n. 1. To cry; scream; make a noise ( chillānā ). 2. To force one's way; thrust oneself in. Ghar meṅ aṛṛāyā chalā ātā hai . 3. To come down in torrents. Āj to pānī aṛṛāë paṛo . Rus. aṛṛā-ke gir paṛnā . v. n. 1. To come down with a crash. Woh peṛ aṛṛa-ke gir paṛā . The tree came down with a crash. 2. To fall upon; attack; assault. Sārī fauj aṛṛā-ke gir paṛī .
v. n. To produce a continued sound (as a mill; the word being an imitation of the sound).

ety: inf. n. of عرش 'to construct a house,' &c.
def: s.m. A roof; a canopy; the highest (the ninth) sphere, the empyrean (where the throne of God is); a throne, chair of state
more: n. m. The 8th or highest heaven where the throne of God is. Chār pāoṅ ko thambak thambā, jā ke pūṅch lagaiyā, Jā pe charh-ke ambar dekhā, tau bhī na maro merī maiyā! Bājā bāje re, mere bhaiyā! Pīpal ke se patte maṅgāë, jā pe lāṛī dahiyā, Sārī rātaṅ lohū thūko, tau bhī na maro, merī maiyā! Bājā bāje re mere bhaiyā! Chār Parī aras se utrīṅ, jin pe lāl rajaiyā, Sārī rātan ḍukkan māro, tau bhī na maro merī maiyā! Bājā bāje re, mere bhaiyā! Chamārs' play. Four legs like pillars thick and eke a tail therefrom hung free, Atop I saw the sky, yet, mother! death came not to me! Drum drum away, my friends! Some leaves like pipal ( pān ) brought to me, with tyre for zest d'you see, All night pure blood I spat, yet, mother! death come not to me! Drum drum away, my friends! Four fairies in red quilts all wrapped descended from the sky, All night they punched me, still, O mother! death came not to me! …
s. m. 1. A roof. 2. A throne. 3. The ninth heaven, where the throne of God is.
subs: v.n. To be very self-conceited; to be proud or haughty
(-ko), To raise (one) to the dais, to seat one in the chair of state; to raise (one) to a high seat or rank, to exalt; to laud or praise to the skies
or ʻarsh-se-le farsh-tak, From the highest heaven to the earth
or ʻarsh-ě-barīn, s.m. The highest heaven, the empyrean.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عرش ('arš), from Arabic عَرْش (ʕarš)۔

def: s.f. Mirror, looking-glass; a small mirror worn, in place of a stone, in a thumb-ring by Indian women, also the ring with the mirror.
more: n. f. S. आ and दृश् to see, Pālī. ādāso . 1. A looking-glass in general (darpan) . Fārsī bolī, "āīnā, 1 " Turkī ḍhūṅdī, "pāīnā, 2 " Hindī bolūṅ "ārsī 3 āë." Khusro kahe, koī na batāë . Riddle. Ans. Ārsī looking-glass. In Persian it is " āīnā ," In Turkī it is " pāīnā ," In Hindī I say " ārsī ." Khusro tells, but none can see. Or thus— The Persian tongue comes not [at all to me], I search in Turkī , but find not (the word). To speak in Hindī, I'm ashamed as 'twere, Khusro has told the word, but none find out. 2. A ring surmounted with a tiny looking glass, worn by women on the thumb. 1. Hāth kaṅgan ko ārsī kyā hai? Prov. To see the bracelet on your arm, needs no looking-glass. 2. Sūraj ko kyā ārsī le-ke dekhtā hai? Prov. ār'sī mas'haf , Pop. ārsī mus'haf . Mah. The marriage ceremony among Mahomedans, when the bridegroom reads a chapter in the Korān , and for the first time sees his wife's…
s. f. A mirror (particularly, a mirror on the thumb).

def: s.m. A certain ceremony in connection with marriage among Hindūs. When the bridegroom first comes to the house of the bride he is received by her relatives, who present to him, moving it circularly round his head, a platter painted and divided into several compartments filled with rice and powder for tilak, and in the middle of which is a lamp made of flour, filled with ghī, and having several wicks lighted.
more: n. m. S. आरात्रिक. The light waved before an idol. Ved. आर to praise. A marriage ceremony. ☞ On the arrival of the bridegroom at the house of the bride, he is received by her relations carrying a tray with some rice and powder for tilak , and a four-wicked lamp made of flour in the middle. Jin kā kareṅ saheli ārtā le le thāl parāt . Marriage. Song.

s. m. A ceremony attending marriage. When the bridegroom first comes to the house of the bride, he is received by her relations, who present to him, and move circularly round his head, a platter painted and divided into several compartments; in the middle of which is a lamp made of flour, filled with ghī and having several wicks lighted.

Lying in ambush or under cover; behind. 2. Across; athwart; against; transversely. 3. Under the pretext or pretence of. āṛ meṅ ā'nā , v. n. To intervene; come between. Phr. To stand in the way of; cross the path of; stop the way; make a stand against. āṛe ān'ā, āṛī ā'nā , v. a. 1. To come between; interpose. 2. To protect; shield; guard; screen. Hāth kā dȳā āṛe āë . Prov. What thou givest, shall be thy shield. āṛe hāth le'nā , v. a. 1. To surround with one's arms; embrace; hug; clasp to one's bosom ( kaulī bharnā ). Āṛe hāthoṅ jo lȳā usko shab …
s. m. A patron, a protector.

Further from Prakrit 𑀅𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀢𑀸𑀮𑀻𑀲 (aṭṭhatālīsa), from Sanskrit अष्टाचत्वारिंशत् (aṣṭā́catvāriṃśat). More information Compare Punjabi ਅਠਤਾਲ਼ੀ (aṭhatāḷī) / اَٹھتاࣇِیہہ (aṭhtāḷīh), which retained the earlier Apabhramsa ठ (ṭha) between अ (a) and त (ta). Also compare Gujarati અડત…

more: n. f. Ved. आर to praise. 1. A ceremony performed in adoration of some god by waving, in a circle before the image, a platter containing a five-wicked burning lamp, flour, and incense. Ārti mach rahī kahīṅ ṭhan, ṭhan, Kahīṅ ghanṭoṅ kī ho rahī chhan chhan . Nazīr. Here ārtī's swung with ding dong dell, There tinkling bells their jingle swell. 2. The same rite, only substituting a bridegroom for the idol, is called ārtā , q. v. 3. A hymn of praise. Jaë Jānkī-Nāthā, tum Raghunāth, hamāre māt, pitā, bhirātā, Tum ho Sajan, saṅghātī, bhagt mukt-dātā, Jaë, deo! Jaë, deo! Hymn ( ārtī ). Reign, Jānkī's Lord, O Raghunāth! mother, father, brother! Husband, and friend! Deliverer of thy worshippers sincere, Reign, god, for ever reign!
subs: ārtī lenā, To perform the ceremony of ārtī.

W. Rus. adv. On this side and the other; right and left. Aryā paryā tāṛ, bīch meṅ sardār . E. On either side, palm trees; in the centre sits the master. (Boy's chaff, supposed to be addressed by one boy seated between two others, who retort— Ore dhore mānak-motī, bīch meṅ gū kī chothī . W. adv. On this side and that; right and left. (See ār-pār.)

ety: Zend arej, S. अर्ज, rt. अर्ह

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ارز (arz).

ety: inf. n. of عرض 'to show the breadth,' 'to show, exhibit, present,' &c.
def: s.f. Presenting or representing; representation, petition, request, address; — (v.n. fr. عرض 'to be broad'), s.m. Breadth, width; (in Geog.) latitude; — a military muster, a review
more: n. m. 1. Breadth; width. 2. ( Geog .) Latitude. 3. A contingent or accidental quality. 4. n. f. Solicitation; suit; appeal (اوداس 1). arz-i-irsāl . A report; return; invoice. arz-ul-balad ; H. paṛā chakkar yā paṛī lakīr , Lines of latitude. arz-begī , (In Eastern Govts.) An officer who presents letters and representations; an usher; the chief petitioner. arz-i-hāl , n. m. A statement; complaint; representation. arz-i-qamar ; S. शार Latitude of the moon. arz k. , v. a. 1. To request; beg; apply; make application. 2. To submit; state humbly. 3. To propose; suggest; move. 4. To report; represent; memorialize. arz-kunindah, arzī dihandah . n. m. Petitioner. arz mārūz . Petition; request; case. arz o tūl ; H. lambāī chauṛāī ; Length and breadth arz tūl umaq , Length, breadth, and depth; dimension ( jisāmat ).

s. f. 1. A petition, request, representation. عرض بيگي aﻌrẓ-beg-ī, An officer who presents letters and representations, an usher. عرضداشت aﻌrẓ-dāsht, s. f. A written petition. عرض کرنا aﻌrẓ karnā, To represent, offer, propose. 2. Breadth, latitude. عرضدار aﻌrẓ-dār, Broad. 3. A military muster. ¶ aﻌraẓ, s. f. 1. An accident, any thing that befalls one, disease, sickness. 2. A muster of troops.
subs: s.m. (In Eastern govts.) An officer who presents and reads letters and representations (to a king or great personage), an usher
s.m. Statement, or representation, of a case, memorial, petition
s.f. A written petition; a memorial; an address
(-kī, or -ko), To make representation (of), to represent, to submit, to state humbly; to report; to memorialize; to make application (for), to apply (for), to request, beg
s.f. The purport or matter of a petition, &c.; petition, request, case
wety: Borrowed from Arabic عَرْض (ʕarḍ).

subs: Lit. 'To hold cheap'; to give away, present, bestow; — arzānī honā, To be granted or bestowed.

def: adj. Relating to the earth; earthly; terrestrial; mundane.

ety: ʻarẓ, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Representation, written statement, petition, letter (from an inferior)
more: n. f. pl. arāiz . A written statement, petition, or representation. Zarā sī bāt pe arzī lagā dī banye ne, Khilāë nauj kisī ko Khudā udhār aisā! Wom. Rāhat. The Baniya for the least thing into Court puts one, God grant that fellows such as these have debtors none! arzī-dāvā , n. m. A petition of plaint; the first pleading in a suit. arzī purzā k. , v. n. To bring an action; sue. arzī fidvī . I have the honor to be, Sir, your most obedient servant; your humble petitioner. arzī guzrānnā, lagānā, tānnā, ṭhoknā, yā denā , v. a. To present a petition; file a plaint. arzī lenā , v. n. 1. To receive a petition. 2. To entertain a case. arzī musanna . A duplicate plaint. arzī-navīs , n. m. A scrivener; one who writes out petitions for suitors; a notary. ٴarāiz aur ahkām , Petitions and orders; correspondence.
s. f. A representation, memorial, petition, letter (from an inferiour).
subs: s.m. Petition of plaint; the first pleading in a suit
v.n. To present or file a petition; to bring an action (against)
or ʻarẓī denā, or ʻarẓī gardānnā, or ʻarẓī lagānā (-ko), To present a petition, to file a plaint
(-kī), To receive a petition; to entertain a case
s.m. One who writes petitions for suitors, a scrivener, a notary.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عرضی ('arzī), ultimately from Arabic عَرْض (ʕarḍ).

def: s.f. Tin; base or light money.

ety: prob. āz-ūr = āz-war, transposed; see āz
def: s.f. Wish, desire, longing, eagerness; hope; trust; expectation; intention, purpose, object, design. inclination, affection, love
more: n. f. 1. Desire ( chāh ); hankering; longing; solicitude; wish; eagerness; thirst; craving ( ichchhā ). 2. Prayer; suit; importunity; supplication; entreaty ( ardās ). Hai ārzū ye hī ki Khudā ādmī * kare, Gar ādmī kare, to bhalā ādmī kare . My prayer to God is this that man he would make me; If man he makes me, then a good man let me be. 3. Hope; trust; expectation ( ās ). 4. Affection; fondness; tenderness ( pyār ). Kis ārzū se to bulāyā, aur is hāl se nikālā. —karā'nā , v. a. To exact much entreaty or hard begging. Thoṛā denā, bahut ārzū karānā . Prov. Small givers, great exactors. ārzū kar'nā , v. a. 1. To wish; desire; fancy; care for ( chāhnā ). 2. To long for; desire greatly; catch or jump at. 3. To desiderate; yearn for; aspire after. 4. To want; need; feel the want of ( māṅgnā ). 5. To solicit; importune; woo; court.
s. f. Wish, desire, hope, want, distress. آرزو کرنا ārzū karnā, To wish, to desire. آرزو کش ārzū-kash, adj. Desirous.
subs: (caus. of next), v.t. To make one wish; to make one expect; to demand or require great eagerness, &c.
(kī), To wish, desire; long for, yearn (after); to want, need
adj. Desirous; solicitous: longing for, craving, auxious; eager; intent on
s.f. Eager desire, wishfulness; solicitude; anxiety.

def: adj. Desirous, longing.
adj. 1. Importunate; wistful; hankering after; dying for. 2. Desirous; solicitous; longing for; craving; anxious; eager; aspiring; intent on; bent on; set upon; concerned.

s.f. Wish, desire, longing; hope, expectation, prospect; trust, reliance; hope or prospect of offspring; children, offspring

ās pūrnā, ās purī honā (-kī), To be gratified (one's desire); to be fulfilled or realized (one's hope, expectation, &c.)
ās ćḥoṛnā or ćhoṛ-denā (-kī), To give up, abandon or relinquish hope

ās ćhūṭnā, v.n. To lose hope, become despondent, to despond; to yield to despair

A mace-bearer ( soṅṭā-bardār ). asāë sultānī , n. m. A royal sceptre. asā o ballam , n. m. 1. Royal insignia. 2. Mace, flags, etc. borne in a marriage procession.

def: s.f. Wish, desire, longing; hope, expectation, prospect; trust, reliance; hope or prospect of offspring; children, offspring
more: n. f. S. आ + शास् to desire, Pālī. āsimsā hope. 1. Wish; desire; longing; solicitude. 2. Hope; expectation; trust; dependence; reliance; faith; assurance. 1. Rauh rī kutyā merī ās, maiṅ āūṅ Kātak mās . Wom. Prov. Look out for me you b—,I'll be back in October. 2. Bakhat, be-bakhat bā kī ās, Rāt dinā wok rahwat pās, Mere man ko karat sab kām, "E sakhī, sājan?" "Nā, sakhī, Rām ." Wom. Mukrī . A riddle. Sunshine and cloud, I look to him, He keeps by me all day and night, And grants me all my heart's delight. A lover, dear?" "No, dear, 'tis Ram." 3. Dānā na ghās, ghoṛe, terī ās . Prov. Nor gram, nor grass (he gives him), yet he trusts the horse will carry him. 4. Rupaīyā-bāre ko rupaīyā kī ās, mo ko Rām kī ās . 3. Protection; shelter; refuge. Mār gusaīāṅ, terī ās . Prov. Slay me, but in thee is my trust. (Addressed to a master or patron—commonly by the aggrieved wife). 4. Hope of issue or of…
s. f. Hope, desire, trust, reliance. (Affixed to other words آس signifies inclination, and آسا possessed of inclination; as پیاس piyās, Thirst, and پیاسا piyāsā, Thirsty, from پي pī, Drink).

v.n. To have hope or trust, be hopeful; to look for; to be expecting.

more: n. f. Pl. of اسم name. 1. Name; office ( ohdah ); appointment; post ( jagah ); situation; berth; employment. 2. A person; individual; party; substitute. Asāmī came to signify an individual from A. āsām (names), the heading of the register in which the names of the cultivators, etc. are recorded. Each item or entry thus became asāmī . 3. ( In law ). An inhabitant; a tenant ( raīyat ); cultivator ( boā jotā ). Asāmī ko gāoṅ se nikāl dȳā . 4. A debtor; witness; culprit; defendant; client. Asāmī ko hāzir karo . 5. A customer; purchaser. 6. A girl; wench; a woman of ill-fame; strumpet; a friend; a lover. 1. Koi bārā baras kī āsāmī lāo . 2. Āj to tum bhī ṭake kī āsāmī ban gaë . 7. People; souls; community; population. asāmī banānā , v. a. To pigeon; bilk; chouse; chisel; fleece; to make a fool of; palm upon. Phr. To make one believe the moon is made of cream and cheese; to practise on one's c…

def: s.m. Sitting; sitting in peculiar postures according to the custom of Jogīs in their devotional exercises (of which eighty-four are enumerated); mode of sitting, posture, attitude, position; seat, place, stool; a small carpet or deer-skin on which Hindūs sit during prayer; the withers of an elephant, the part where the driver sits; driver's seat; rider's seat (on a horse); the inside or under part of the thigh; a market-stall; — maintaining a post against an enemy
more: n. m. Pālī. āsanam . S. आस् to sit. 1. Posture; attitude; position; the mode of sitting. 2. The mode of sitting of jogīs at their devotional exercises, of which they enumerate eighty-four. 1. Āsan birah kā mār, bhabūt ishq kī chaṛhā. Maṭh meṅ birah ke mujh kūṅ sanāsī kȳā, pȳā . Walī. 2. Kuë par āsan jogī kā, jal bharūṅ; ki rītī jāūṅ . Wom. Song. The jogī is seated by the well, shall I fill my pitcher, or go back with it empty? 3. The driver's seat; the rider's seat (on a horse); the fork. 4. A small carpet on which Hindūs sit at prayer, also called āsnī . 5. One of thirty-six postures described in Kok Shāstar —branded as carnal and unmentionable; the thigh. āsan tal'e ānā , v. n. 1. To be ridden, or mounted (a horse). 2. To suffer carnal intercourse. āsan jamā'nā , v. n. 1. To have a good grip; to stick, or sit well (on a horse). Ghoṛan kāṭhī ghāl lo, chaukas āsan lo jamāë . Rus. Song.…

s. m. 1. A stool, a seat, a small carpet on which the Hindūs sit at prayer, a carpet to sit on. 2. Stay, abiding, posture, attitude, sitting (especially, the attitudes adopted by jogīs in their devotional exercises, of which they enumerate eighty-four). 3. The inside or under part of the thigh. 4. The withers of an elephant, the part where the driver sits. آسن تلي آنا āsan tale ānā, To come under subjection. آسن حوڙنا āsan joṛnā, To sit on the hams. آسن ڐولنا āsan ḍolnā, Spoken of a holy man, who perceiving by supernatural intelligence that some one in distress has called on him, interposes in his behalf. آسن سي آسن جوڙنا āsan se āsan joṛnā, To sit close in contact with another person. آسن لگانا āsan lagānā, To sit obstinately in a given posture till oneʼs request be obtained. آسن مارنا āsan mārnā, To sit (particularly in an attitude practised by jogīs or devotees), to retain oneʼs seat …
s. m. The name of a tree on which the tasar silk-worm feeds. (Terminalia alata tomentosa).
subs: To come under the thighs, to be ridden or mounted (a horse), to come under subjection; to be embraced in carnal intercourse
v.n. To have a good grip with the thighs (on a horse), to sit firmly, stick (on)
āsan joṛke baiṭhnā, āsan-se āsan joṛnā, v.n. To sit on the hams; to squat on the ground like a Jogī; to sit in close contact (with, lit. 'thigh to thigh')
(-ke), To shake the firm-sitting or position (of), to shake the virtue (of); to allure, tempt, seduce
To move or interpose in behalf of (said of a holy man who perceives by supernatural intelligence that some one in distress has invoked his aid)
(-ke), To sit down, settle, take up (one's) abode, abide; tarry; to sit obstinately in one place and position till one's demands are satisfied
(-par), āsan mār-ke baiṭhnā (-par), To sit on the haunches (like a Jogī), to squat; to sit in one posture without change; to have a firm seat (on a horse).

ety: fr. āsā, rt. of āsūdan; see āsūda
def: adj. Easy, facile; smooth; light; feasible; manageable; convenient, commodious; unencumbered, disencumbered (affair)
more: adj. 1. Easy ( sahj ); facile; light; feasible; smooth; manageable. Nīat sābut, manzil āsān . Prov. A firm resolve, an easy journey. 2. Unencumberd; disencumbered (affair). āsān kar'nā , v. a. 1. To render easy; to facilitate; smooth; lighten; bridge over. 2. To free; clear; disencumber; clear the way; disentangle; unclog; unravel. 3. To disengage; free from; disburden. āsān ho'nā , v. n. 1. To be easy; practicable. 2. To flow; swim or drift with the tide or stream. Phr. To have it all one's own way; to walk over the course; to make nothing of; to go with the stream.
adj. Easy, convenient, commodious, facile. آسان کرنا āsān karnā, To facilitate, expedite.
subs: v.t. To render easy; to facilitate, expedite, smooth, lighten; to clear away, disencumber, free (from), disentangle
(-par), To be easy (to or for); to be practicable.

ety: pl. of ثار, the arabicised form of सेर ser
def: s.m. Seers; seer (ser, 'a weight of two pounds').
s.m. Footprint; sign, mark, token, trace, track, vestige, shadow; impress, impression, influence; effect; result, consequence
more: n. m. A mark made on the sides of the feet of camels, by the impression of which upon the ground they can be traced when straying in the desert. 1. Impression; effect; influence. Tukhm tāsīr sohbat kā asar . Prov. Society moulds men; as the seed, so the tree. 2. Result; issue; fruit; consequence. asar kar'nā , v. a. 1. To have, take, or produce effect; to effect; to be effective; to operate. 2. To stimulate; excite (one's passions); move (one's feelings); melt; touch; affect; to give a stimulus to. asar ho'nā , v. n. 1. To prevail; have effect; make an impression. 2. To be affected, impressed, influenced. Hazār samjhāte haiṅ, par khāk asar nahiṅ hotā .
n. m. تاج 1. A crown; diadem; tiara 2. A high crowned cap. 3. A crest; the comb of a bird. 4. A suit in cards. tāj-dār , n. m. A crowned head; a king. sar-tāj . A head; chief; master; lord.
subs: To have or take effect (on), to prove efficacious, to effect, be effective; to operate; to make an impression; to affect, move, touch; to stimulate; to excite (the passions)
An effect to be produced, to have effect (on, -meṅ); to prevail.

def: n. m. pl. of اثر 1. Signs; marks; token; traces; impressions; vestiges. Āj to meṅh ke se āsār haiṅ . 2. Preliminary or premonitory signs; the promises (of youth or childhood). Laṛke ke āsār to achchhe haiṅ, āge Khudā jāne . 3. State; condition; symptoms. Dāddā ke asār achchhe nahīṅ, bāpūe bol le . Rus. 4. The traditions of Muhammad; sacred relics. 5. The foundation of a building; the breadth of a wall. 6. A standard weight of one seer. āsār-i-sharīf,—mubārak , Ped. n. m. 1. Sacred relics of Muhammad. 2. The four associates or companions of Muhammad, afterwards his successors.
s. m. plur. (of اٿر) Signs, marks, traces, impressions, vestiges, effects; histories, traditions, memorable events; the traditions of Muḥammad.
s. m. A mark, sign, trace, impression, effect. اٿر کرنا as̤ar karnā, To operate, affect.
